title: gentle smoked greek smoked-mackerel dumplings
description: romantic greek dish featuring smoked-mackerel, fluffy dumplings, best served on a robata grill.

ingredients.name: dried all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: use high-quality flour for best results

ingredients.name: smoked-mackerel fillets
ingredients.notes: boneless and skinless for easier handling

ingredients.name: chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: add fresh herbs at the end for best flavor

ingredients.name: chopped scallions
ingredients.notes: use both green and white parts for added flavor

ingredients.name: lemon zest
ingredients.notes: use organic lemons for best flavor

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use high-quality sea salt for best flavor

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: use fresh peppercorns for best flavor

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: use high-quality extra-virgin oil for best flavor

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, warm water, and a pinch of salt. Mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes elastic and smooth.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Use a rolling pin to flatten each ball into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of ricotta cheese in the center of each circle.
Add a few pieces of smoked-mackerel on top of the ricotta cheese.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Heat a robata grill to medium-high heat and brush with olive oil.
Cook the dumplings for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Serve the dumplings hot with a drizzle of olive oil and a sprinkle of parsley and scallions.
